source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Five children should paint three quarters of the total amount of the little squares on their trays. One of the children A, B, C, D or E was wrong. Which one?
<image1>
extraction_notes: options field contains only labels A-E, no descriptive option text
answer field is a label and corresponding option text is also just the same label

image_dependency: question asks which child A-E was wrong about painting three quarters of the little squares on their trays, which requires seeing each tray
image_manifest shows 1 available image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving likely requires evaluating multiple candidates (A, B, C, D, E) rather than reading a single visible fact
the phrase 'three quarters of the total amount of the little squares' implies counting squares and checking a fraction condition
multiple trays/children must be compared to identify the one incorrect child
verifiability: answer_type is choice/option with a single-letter answer_text='C'
the target condition is precise: whether each child painted three quarters of the squares
registry_passed=true and the image has a nontrivial readability_score (0.7705), supporting downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantics are well supported
asset registry passed with no recorded issues, indicating good asset completeness
image dependency is very high, making this a strong fit for image-based evaluation
multi-step and verifiability scores are both strong, suggesting useful and checkable problem-solving value
no risk flags were reported in the initial scoring record
